subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + Turkish official (CBRT; TurkStat) cross-checked against independent estimates + independent/exile Turkish press (Diken, Bianet, Medyascope, Duvar, Turkish Minute).

Source discipline: the official inflation series is DISPUTED - scored on the falling trajectory with confidence capped and statistical integrity rated clearly negative; pro-government captured media excluded on politics/press/rule-of-law.
Standout drags: a politicised judiciary and the 2025 opposition-mayor arrest, very low press freedom, scarred monetary credibility, and a catastrophic Istanbul seismic tail.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May — the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A genuine orthodox re-stabilisation and a diversified export base, against scarred monetary credibility and disputed inflation data.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt low and sustainable (~25-30% GDP) but rising, deficit ~3.6-3.9%, with large quasi-fiscal and earthquake-reconstruction liabilities. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An orthodox turn (sharp rate hikes, the FX-protected-deposit scheme wound down) but statutory independence is fragile and scarred by 2021-23 governor firings. (CBRT; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The lira is not a reserve currency. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: An energy importer already fighting high inflation, Turkey is further squeezed by the 2026 Hormuz energy-price shock. (Reuters)

banking_stability.notes: Stress-tested through the lira crisis with adequate capital and the deposit-scheme exit cutting balance-sheet risk; fragile and dollarized. (IMF; CBRT)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A diversified manufacturing/export base, an EU customs union, and a geographic hub role; a chronic current-account/energy-import drag, no resource curse. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A capable digital bureaucracy, overwhelmed by a politicised judiciary, disputed statistics, and heavy media capture.

rule_of_law.notes: A politicised judiciary used against the opposition; low WJP standing. (WJP; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: The official inflation series is disputed - independent estimates run higher - reflecting a credibility gap and post-2022 board interference. (ENAG)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable bureaucracy and strong digital government (e-Devlet), undercut by politicised appointments. (World Bank; OECD)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 163/180, with dozens of journalists jailed and the great majority of media government-aligned. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: Serious democratic backsliding and restricted liberties, with a stalled Kurdish peace process the one structural positive.

political_stability.notes: Consolidated rule, but the March 2025 arrest of Istanbul's mayor (the lead opposition figure) and the largest protests in over a decade mark serious backsliding. (BalkanInsight; Time)

civil_liberties.notes: Restricted assembly and speech with mass detentions in the 2025-26 protests; rated 'Not Free.' (HRW; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful citizenship-by-investment (property) and residence routes are durable, but anti-refugee sentiment and tightening are rising. (official; Bianet)

social_cohesion.notes: A 2025 PKK self-dissolution/disarmament process (a positive) but stalled, against the Kurdish question, anti-refugee sentiment, and polarisation. (OSW)

demographics.notes: A relatively young population but with fertility ~1.5 and falling - ageing ahead. (TurkStat; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A transactional NATO member hedging Russia, with active regional operations and intervention risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO member but a transactional one hedging Russia (the S-400 purchase, energy ties). (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: Syria/PKK operations, Eastern Mediterranean tensions, an active earthquake zone, and regional spillover. (ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A history of FX interventions; the deposit scheme is unwound but intervention risk persists. (CBRT; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Strong healthcare and modern western infrastructure, against a catastrophic seismic mega-hazard and water stress.

climate.notes: Mediterranean drought/heat intensification, water stress, wildfire, with relatively high adaptive capacity. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Near-universal coverage, decent quality, and a medical-tourism hub. (OECD; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Modern in the west and improving, with quake-exposed building-code-enforcement gaps. (World Bank)

disaster_insurance.notes: A mandatory earthquake scheme (DASK) exists but with large coverage caps and incomplete uptake. (DASK)

summary: A diversified manufacturing economy mid-way through a genuine orthodox re-stabilisation - sharp rate hikes, the FX-protected-deposit scheme wound down, inflation falling from extreme highs - sitting on a low debt base and an EU customs union. But the dominant drags are institutional and political: a politicised judiciary, the 2025 arrest of the lead opposition figure and the protests it triggered, very low press freedom, and disputed official inflation data, all atop a catastrophic Istanbul seismic tail. The 2025 Kurdish disarmament process is the one structural positive on social cohesion.
